title: )Selamba Gounder vs )Ranganathan on 30 April, 2014
body: )Selamba Gounder vs )Ranganathan on 30 April, 2014 Author: S.Manikumar Bench: S.Manikumar BEFORE the HIGH COURT OF JUDICATURE AT MADRAS DATED : 30.04.2014 CORAM : THE HONOURABLE MR.JUSTICE S.MANIKUMAR CRP.No.2282 of 2010 and Connected Miscellaneous Petitions 1)Selamba Gounder 2)Verammal ... Petitioners Vs. 1)Ranganathan 2)Seerangan 3)Madesh 4)Venkatachalam 5)Thangathammal 6)Seerangammal 7)Madhammal 8)Rajendran 9)Vellachi 10)Govindammal 11)Palanisamy 12)Lakshmi 13)Periasamy 14)Kavitha 15)Chinnappan 16)Irusammal 17)Govindasamy 18)Asokan 19)Perumayee 20)Gnanamani 21)Thiagarajan 22)Punithavathy 23)Govindarajan 24)Ravi 25)Kavitha 26)Senthil 27)Ganesan 28)Sankar 29)Subramanian 30)Shanmugavalli 31)Lakshmanan 32)Ramanathan alias Thangavel 33)Murugan 34)Venkittu 35)Vajravel 36)Iyyamperumal 37)Kuttiyannan 38)Chinnasamy 39)Govindammal 40)Chinnaponnu 41)Sundari 42)Sengodan ... Respondents (Respondents 8 to 42 are given-up) Prayer : Petition filed under Section 115 of the Civil Procedure Code, against the fair and decreetal order of the learned I Additional Sub Judge, Salem, dated 29.04.2010 made in REP.No.263/2007 in O.S.No.319/1976. For Petitioner : Mr.R.Subramanian For Respondents 1 to 7 : Mr.M.Vivekandan For Respondents 8 to 42: Given-up O R D E R Civil Revision Petition is filed against the order made in REP.263/07 in O.S.319/1976 dated 29.04.2010 on the file of the learned I Additional Sub Judge, Salem. For the sake of convenience, the parties will be referred as per their rankings in the Execution Petition. 2. The Civil Revision Petition is filed by the defendants/respondents 47 and 48 in E.P.263/2007 in the above said suit and the respondents are the plaintiffs and other legal representatives. The plaintiffs have obtained a final decree in the above said suit on 21.11.2003 pursuant to a preliminary decree dated 25.10.2002. 3. According to the plaintiffs they are entitled to get separate possession of the petition mentioned properties. The respondents 1 to 23 in the Execution Petition are the legal representatives of the respondents 11 and 12. The respondents 24 to 32 are not willing to give separate possession. 4. The 33rd respondent has filed a counter and it is adopted by respondents 34 to 39. According to these respondents 33, 34 to 39, the description of the property as stated in the Execution Petition, is entirely wrong. The decree holders have got supplementary preliminary decree in their favour for 53 cents in the total extent of 1.06 acres in Survey No.62/14, but in the Execution Petition, the petitioners wanted delivery of possession by mentioning he survey numbers as 62/4B2. 5. According to the respondents, survey No.62/4 and 62/4B2 are entirely different. The Tahsildar of Omalur has deposed before the lower court that survey Nos.62/4 and 62/4B2 are not one and the same. The respondents have submitted that they are the bona fide purchasers of properties in Survey No.62/4B2 from its lawful owners and that they have filed O.S.No.268/2004 for declaration of title and permanent injunction in the court of District Munsif, Omalur and the suit is pending. The petitioner in the Execution Petition has entered appearance in the suit. According to the respondents, the lower court has no jurisdiction to entertain the execution petition. According to the respondents, the decree itself is forged and hence, not executable. 6. The learned I Additional Sub Judge, Salem, allowed this REP.263/2007 for delivery of possession of the properties mentioned in the Execution Petition. The Execution Petition has been filed under Section 50 Order 21 Rule 11, 35 and Section 151 CPC, for delivery of possession by removing any person from the petition mentioned properties. According to the decree holders, a portion of the property allotted to the decree holders was allegedly purchased by the respondents 33 to 48, the legal heirs of the deceased respondents 11 and 12 and that the subsequent purchasers have been added, as respondents 24 to 32 and 33 to 48. 7. The 27th respondent has filed a counter and it was adopted by respondents 2, 14,17, 19, 22, 24 to 26, 28, 29 to 32. These respondents admit the claim of the petitioners. 8. A separate counter has been filed by 21st respondent and it is also adopted by 13th respondent. So, according to this respondent, the decree holders have got a Supplementary Preliminary decree, in their favour for 53 cents of land, in the total extent of 1.06 acres in survey No.62/4, but, now the decree holders have filed Execution Petition for delivery of possession by mentioning the survey Nos.62/4B2. According to these respondents, they have purchased 81= cents in Survey No.62/4B2 on 08.09.1998, under a registered sale deed from Vedappan s/o. Vedichi Gounder and that they have sold the said property to the respondents 33 to 48 under different sale deeds. According to these respondents, the said Kaliappa Gounder had never purchased the property in survey No.62/4 or 62/4B2 and so the decree holders cannot claim any right in respect of the properties which did not belong to Kaliappa Gounder. 9. The 33rd respondent has filed counter and adopted by respondents 34 to 39. According to these respondents, the decree holders have got a supplementary preliminary decree in their favour for 53 cents from the total extent of 1.06 acres in survey No.62/4 whereas, the decree holders have come forward in this execution petition for delivery of possession by mentioning the survey number as 62/4B2. According to these respondents, the survey No.62/4B2 are entirely different. The Tahsildar of Omalur has deposed that survey No.62/4 and 62/4B2 are not one and the same. These respondents submit that they are the bonafide purchasers of the property situated in survey No.62/4B2. According to the respondents, unless the declaratory suit is disposed off, the execution petition cannot be allowed. Further, according to them, the lower court has jurisdiction to entertain the execution petition. 10. A separate counter has been filed by the 44th respondent and it has been adopted by respondents 40, 42 to 48, so according to these respondents, the decree holders have got a supplementary preliminary decree for 53 cents in the total extent of 1.06 acres in survey No.62/4, but they filed execution petition for delivery of possession by mentioning the survey numbers as 62/4B2. These respondents have submitted that survey No.62/4 and 62/4B2 are entirely different with different boundaries, and these respondents are the bonafide purchasers of property in survey No.62/4B2. According to these respondents, the decree itself is forged. A separate counter was filed by 47th respondent and adopted by 38th respondent. These respondents have purchased the property in survey No.64/5B. The decree holders have sold a portion of the property to Palani Gounder. According to them, when possession was already given, decree holders cannot pray for possession in this Execution Petition. 11. A separate counter has been filed by the 33th respondent and it is adopted by respondents 34 to 46. The final decree was passed in I.A.No.59/2003 in O.S.No.319/1976 in respect of property in survey No.62/14 but these respondents purchased property in survey No.62/4B2 from the rightful owners. These respondents have submitted that the survey Nos.61/4 are entirely different by extent and boundary. These respondents also prayed for dismissal of E.P. 12. The executing court has framed the point for consideration as "whether the petition is to be allowed or not. The decree holders have examined PW1 to PW3 and marked Exs.P1 to P3. On the side of the respondents, RW1 to RW3 have been examined and Exhibits B1 to B46 have been marked. In the final decree, A schedule items have been allotted to the decree holders. Respondents 2, 14, 17, 22, 24 to 32 have admitted the claim of the decree holders. The respondents 13 and 21 in the Execution Petition have filed a counter and that the 21st respondent has been examined as RW1. Ex.B1 to B21 have been marked through RW2 and respondents 33 to 46 have filed their counter and 36th respondent has been examined. The respondents 47 and 48 have filed their counter and that the 47th respondent has been examined as RW2. Ex.B22 to B29 have been marked through RW2. The contesting respondents have stated that the decree in O.S.319/ 76 is not valid and the Sub Court, Salem, has no territorial jurisdiction to try the suit. It is their case that the decree holders have sold their property on 13.03.2006 and the decree obtained by them by fraud. According to the Commissioner, the extent of survey No.62/4B2 is 81=. Regarding the dispute in the property, a suit O.S.268/2004 is pending in Munsif Court at Omalur, the respondents 33 to 46 purchased the property in survey No.62/4B2. 13. According to the contesting respondents, the execution petition is barred by limitation. Suit in O.S.319/ 76 has ended with a final decree on 21.11.2003. The Execution Petition was filed in 2007. So the Execution Petition is in time. Respondents 13 to 21 in the Execution Petition have filed a petition under Section 47 CPC., in REA.302/2008, raising all these defence and that petition was dismissed after a detailed enquiry. The contesting respondents viz., 13 to 21 are the parties in the final decree application and that they remained ex parte. 'A' schedule property has been clearly demarcated in the plan enclosed with the final decree. None of the respondents in the final decree have objected to the final decree and they have not filed any appeal. The contention of the respondents 33 to 46 is that they had purchased the properties from the respondents 13 to 21 during the pendency of the proceedings. The Executing Court has carefully analyzed oral and documentary evidence and held that there is no flaw in the execution proceedings and so disagreed with the objections raised by the respondents who have filed their counter. 14. This Court has heard the learned counsel for the petitioner and also the learned counsel for the respondents and I do not find any illegality or error or justification in the order passed by the lower court. 15. In the result, the Civil Revision Petition is dismissed. In the circumstances, there is no order as to cost. Consequently, connected miscellaneous petitions are closed. 30.04.2014 skm To I Additional Sub Judge, Salem. S.MANIKUMAR, J. skm CRP(NPD)No.2282 of 2010 and Connected Miscellaneous Petitions 30.09.2014
